rationale.note: All 4 pool entries are Open Library false positives — none relate to transformer/attention in ML sense. 'Cartesian Meditations' (Husserl), 'The Origin of Ideas' (Rosmini), 'History of the World's Fair' (Truman 1893), 'Understanding Events' (Zacks 2007) are philosophy/history. The books engine returned 0 ML-relevant results for this query; the pool passes the BOOK_WHITELIST filter but is entirely off-topic. Including all 4 per undersized-pool rule but Jaccard scores from this pair are noise. This reveals a books-mode precision problem for ML/DL queries — the query modifier +book is insufficient to surface relevant technical ML books through the current engine set.
